steps.dialogue: Welcome back! In our last session, we explored fractions in new ways.

We learned that equivalent fractions—like [fraction numerator=1 denominator=2]one half[/fraction] and [fraction numerator=2 denominator=4]two fourths[/fraction]—land at the exact same position on a number line. When fractions share the same spot, they're equal!

We also discovered that some fractions equal whole numbers. When the top and bottom numbers match—like [fraction numerator=4 denominator=4]four fourths[/fraction] or [fraction numerator=3 denominator=3]three thirds[/fraction]—the fraction equals 1.
Today, we'll keep building on these ideas. You're ready to take on new challenges with fractions!
